title: GitHub previews an agent-native Copilot desktop app for managing AI development work
friendlyTitle: GitHub is building a desktop app for managing coding agents
summary: GitHub introduced the Copilot app, a technical preview desktop experience for managing agent sessions, issues, pull requests, automations and isolated worktrees. The app is positioned as a control center for agentic software development.
friendlySummary: GitHub’s Copilot app helps developers see agent sessions, code changes and pull requests in one place. It is designed for teams using several AI coding agents at once.
whyItMatters: As coding agents do more work in parallel, developers need ways to track what is running, what changed and where human review is required. A dedicated agent workspace can reduce the chaos of switching between chats, terminals, branches and pull requests.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can make AI-assisted coding easier to manage and review instead of feeling scattered across many windows.
